summary: The functionalis layer of the endometrium is shed during menstruation, while the basalis layer regenerates it.
The basalis layer is shed during menstruation, while the functionalis layer regenerates.
Both endometrial layers are shed completely during menstruation.
The LH surge triggers ovulation on approximately day 14 of a 28-day cycle.
The FSH surge triggers ovulation on approximately day 21 of a 28-day cycle.
Progesterone surge triggers ovulation on approximately day 7 of the cycle.
Spermatogenesis occurs in seminiferous tubules and takes approximately 74 days.
Spermatogenesis occurs in the epididymis and takes approximately 28 days.
Spermatogenesis occurs in the rete testis and takes approximately 120 days.
Fertilisation normally occurs in the ampulla of the fallopian tube.
Fertilisation normally occurs in the isthmus of the fallopian tube.
Fertilisation normally occurs in the uterine cavity.
The acrosome reaction enables sperm to penetrate the zona pellucida.
The cortical reaction enables sperm to penetrate the zona pellucida.
The capacitation reaction dissolves the zona pellucida.
The corpus luteum secretes progesterone to maintain the endometrium during early pregnancy.
The corpus luteum secretes oestrogen to trigger ovulation.
The corpus luteum secretes FSH to stimulate follicular development.
Primary oocytes are arrested in prophase of meiosis I until puberty.
Primary oocytes are arrested in metaphase of meiosis II until fertilisation.
Primary oocytes complete meiosis during fetal life before birth.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review gametogenesis and fertilisation and try again.
